title: delphinium hybridum subsp. schmalhausenii
intro: Delphinium hybridum subsp. schmalhausenii, also known as the Schmalhausen delphinium or giant larkspur, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, characterized by its tall spikes of vibrant purple, blue, or white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are thought to be due to the presence of alkaloids such as delphinine. Consuming the plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, and it has been used to treat conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be taken internally or applied topically to the skin.
